svg.text: GENERAL NOTES + DDOT REQUIREMENTS
Conditions evaluated for this site — notes whose conditions do not apply are omitted, not crossed out. Site-specific notes appear on each drawing sheet.
DDOT / ADA REQUIREMENTS
1. Platform rides on adjustable pedestals / leveling feet for positive drainage; deck is NOT
bolted or anchored into the roadway (§4.4). Composite decking on PT 2x4 sleepers — the
assembly both approved sets use. [§4.4]
2. Entry flush with the sidewalk: removable 3/8" steel transition plate at the curb, max 3/8"
rise, gap ≤ 1/2" (§4.4; Queen's English self-imposed 3/8" max). [§4.4]
3. Maintain 1'-0" clear stormwater channel along the curb under the deck, with open ends
(§4.6). Construction Details must show positive drainage flow AND how the channel is
accessed/cleared if blocked (§5.2 item 4). [§4.6 + §5.2 item 4]
4. Mark all utilities in/under/adjacent to the streatery per APWA color code before
installation; show proposed markings on the platform/barriers in the Utility Access Plan (§5.2
item 5; operator contact table in Appendix 5). [§5.2 item 5, Appendix 5]
5. Two 5.5"×8.5" signs, one near each end of the sidewalk-facing edge: business name, seating
hours, seating capacity, emergency contact (§4.7, Appendix 1 format). No other signage, logos,
advertising, or branding permitted. [§4.7, Appendix 1]
6. Enclosure tops out 32-42" above the roadway (42" cap rail, both approved sets); gaps
between intermediate members ≤ 19" (§4.2). [§4.2]
7. Overhead structure 8-12 ft above platform; translucent, minimal-profile roof sloped 2:12
(10%) SLOPE TO CURB; NO enclosure above barrier height at any time; vertical members above 42"
8. Concrete blocks required in the 2 ft travel-side buffer (§4.2). DDOT standard detail to
appear on the details sheet (A2.01 equivalent, M5). [§4.2]
9. DDOT-provided Jersey barrier carries reflectors per the Appendix 3 standard detail (signed
by DDOT's chief engineer) — embed on the details sheet rather than redrawing. [Appendix 3]
10. ≥ 1 ADA-accessible table: 28-34" surface height, 27" knee clearance, 30"×48" clear floor
space, on an accessible route (§4.8). All seating 1 ft back from barriers/enclosure. [§4.8]
SITE-WALK VERIFICATIONS
1. Provide access panels / removable planks over any manhole, vault lid, or utility cover
within the footprint (§5.2 item 5). SITE-WALK: locate covers — utility vault coverage in DC
Open Data is incomplete. [§5.2 item 5]
OPERATOR ITEMS
1. IF portable propane heaters are planned: "Propane Use: Heating — Portable Outdoor" permit
from FEMS (fems.dc.gov, ~5 calendar days). Where a roof is present, confirm heater clearances
with FEMS before purchase. [FEMS]

g.text: AUTOMATED DRAFT — generated by streatery pre-screener v3.
Architect review, refinement, and seal required before submission.

svg.text: AUTOMATED DRAFT — ARCHITECT REVIEW REQUIRED
